title: Unable to Help
wikitext:
[]: Unable to Help?             Quote Post by Killericon » Sun Jan 03, 2010 4:09 pm Hi! I'm new here, and you lot are all very intimidating. I thought that I had thought eva out well, but it seems not. Anyways, I was perusing the article for Shinji and I read this: This, paired with the fact that Shinji had killed what he saw as a human being, was part of what drove Shinji into his state of absolute despair in Episode 25', in which he declared that he felt unworthy even of the piloting which had consumed his identity, thinking that if all he did was hurt and kill others, it was better to do nothing. By that, though, he may have condemned Asuka, who was fighting against the Mass Production Evangelions, to death.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Jan 03, 2010 4:39 pm I disagree with that quote; Unit 01 got her ass in gear when she was good and ready, and no sooner. It was too late for Asuka, but saving her wasn't really on Yui's agenda at that point.

[]: Re: Unable to Help?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 03, 2010 4:43 pm Killericon wrote: Is this little snippet simply saying that if Shinji weren't hugging his knees so much, he could've gotten to the cage before the Bakelite and gone out and kicked some ass? I guess the big question I'm asking is was Shinji in a position, at any point, to prevent the horrible things that happened to Asuka? I'm of the opinion that he wasn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 03, 2010 4:54 pm Shin-seiki wrote: I disagree with that quote; Unit 01 got her ass in gear when she was good and ready, and no sooner. It was too late for Asuka, but saving her wasn't really on Yui's agenda at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jan 03, 2010 5:20 pm Once you get to know us, you'll quickly realise we're about as clueless as everyone else. There's an opinion held in some quarters that Shinji has simply become despondent again upon encountering the slightest obstacle and has "left Asuka to die". While there might be elements of this, I'm inclined to think that Shinji has simply become straightforwardly stuck. During Asuka's fight with the MP Evas, we here her state over the radio Episode #25' wrote: Asuka (on speaker) : Jeeez! Don't they ever stop!? And I can't even count on that idiot Shinji!!

[]: Re: Unable to Help?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 03, 2010 5:50 pm Killericon wrote: Is this little snippet simply saying that if Shinji weren't hugging his knees so much, he could've gotten to the cage before the Bakelite

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jan 03, 2010 5:56 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Once you get to know us, you'll quickly realise we're about as clueless as everyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 8:13 pm OMF's post is pretty definitive with regards to Shinji's complete inability regardless of his intent or will to help Asuka. The big debate then shifts to his will and intent. The first time he hears that Asuka is still alive is in the car and you see him give a kind of start and yelp "Asuka!?". But then at the cages he stalls and breaks down and even with Misato trying to spur him on he doesn't want to move. Many argue that at this point he wasn't aware that he couldn't get to 01 and help Asuka so what he's doing is incredibly selfish because while he's moping he knows Asuka is fighting for her life. It's a legitimate point, and yet I still argue that Shinji's mental breakdown/shutdown is entirely justifiable. By this point he is in such a funk that it would've taken more than Misato or Asuka to pull him out of it. But Shinji becoming part of 3I was inevitable because Yui refused to have it any other way.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Mon Jan 04, 2010 12:18 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: blah blah evidence01 blah blah evidence 02 blah blah blah evidence03

[]:             Quote Post by Killericon » Mon Jan 04, 2010 2:17 am Setting aside for a moment the entire happenings of what came before, once Shinji GOT to the cage, does anyone think that he wasn't going to get in? Also, I thought that the UN's radio call about the Bakelite infusion not being a problem was a reference to Misato's filling the halls with Bakelite so as to stall them, and their being able to get around it. What makes you think it was the UN that filled the cage with Bakelite, and not Misato's order?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jan 04, 2010 12:28 pm Killericon wrote: Setting aside for a moment the entire happenings of what came before, once Shinji GOT to the cage, does anyone think that he wasn't going to get in?

[]:             Quote Post by Killericon » Mon Jan 04, 2010 12:41 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: He eventually did after all, and moreover is shown to be frustrated at his inability to help Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jan 04, 2010 3:47 pm Killericon wrote: In any event, so the consensus is that Shinji is petrified by, you know, Shinji-ness, and in this way could be said to have not saved Asuka. However, the Bakelite infusion and Yui would have stopped him anyways?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 04, 2010 3:47 pm Killericon wrote: In any event, so the consensus is that Shinji is petrified by, you know, Shinji-ness, and in this way could be said to have not saved Asuka. However, the Bakelite infusion and Yui would have stopped him anyways?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2010 5:58 pm Azathoth wrote: Shinji would still not have gotten there in time to give Asuka any serious help.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 04, 2010 6:42 pm NemZ wrote: There's almost no way his presence would have been a bad thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Webster » Mon Jan 04, 2010 7:15 pm bladerj wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: blah blah evidence01 blah blah evidence 02 blah blah blah evidence03

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 04, 2010 7:22 pm She'd have to die in order to be in Instrumentality though (besides, how could she somehow survive not just the spear to her eye and arm, but those multiple spear-sewerage when Kyoko clearly died?). And the fact of the matter is, reverting to LCL involves losing your physical form, which in turn is equal to physical death.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 04, 2010 7:44 pm Dr. Webster wrote: You know, I'm probably gonna get flamed for this, but I don't actually believe Asuka dies in EoE. [...] I prefer to think she got knocked unconscious, then was Complemented just like the bridge bunnies.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 04, 2010 8:22 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: It wouldn't be a bad thing of course, but the problem is Yui and Lilith/Adam were calling the shots. Shinji was for the most part along for the ride until any decisions had to be made.
